ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Ki tiŋaŋ kur mun loon lɔɔnŋanae; ki tibiiuk kur mun loon lɔɔnbiite.
translated: The elephant is bigger than the buffalo; the elephant is stronger than the buffalo.

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Yisa nba din wann u poorpoweiteeb piik nan bannlee na wannu maŋ ki gbenn yoonba, ki u nyii leŋ ki saan bi doi ni ki mɔɔntir Yennu maan ki want niib.
translated: The chief's son went to the poor man's farm and saw a lot of yams, so he decided to steal them, but the poor man caught him and took him to the chief's palace.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Ki Yennu jiantu yudamm nan saakab maŋ kur yabir bi mɔŋ paak a bin la faak siara damm mɔb ni linba ki bi sa jii Yisa biit ki kpiu.
translated: They said that the chief's son had gone to the farm and had been bitten by a snake, and they took him to the hospital but he died, and they buried him, and now the chief is crying.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Ki Yisa ŋamm yetib a “Barmɔniie ki n beeri na, Yennu sa nyik chab nifosaarii bi biit nan bi barbiit kura;
translated: When the sun rises, the Barmɔniie people say, "Today is not a day for playing, it is a day for working and farming."

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Asor din mar Sadok, ki Sadok din mar Akim, ki Akim mun din mar Eliud.
translated: The father of Sadok is Asor, and the father of Sadok is Akim, and the father of Akim is Eliud.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Yimm mɔtana nigbouŋ na mun naan nan waas maŋe, kimaan Jɔɔnn daan baar ki ki di jemɔntii, ki bia ki nyu daami, ŋaan ki i yaa a u waate;
translated: They went to the farm and worked, but John did not go, he stayed at home, and they did not see him until they returned.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Ŋanne ki u poorpoweiteeb boiɔ a “Bee ki sennu wannteeb yaa a see ki Yennu sɔkinii Elaija baar Masia sinsinni?”
translated: Why did the poor widow's son ask, "Can't we see the God of Elijah in the God of Moses?"

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: Ki u gbanant din nyirii kaman nan sanyikintii nee, ki u liant peen kaman nan sanpagbanpeeuŋ na.
translated: When you are going to the farm, don't forget to take your cutlass, and when you are going to the bush, don't forget to take your gun.

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Ŋanne ki Yisa din nyik nibur maŋ leŋ, ŋaan saan kɔɔ u ŋadɔɔtik ni. Ki u poorpoweiteeb din baar u boor ni ki tan betɔ a “Betirit dinatimii barjouk na paak.”
translated: When Jesus was born, there was a great celebration in the town. The angels sang a song saying, "Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, goodwill towards men."

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Wa din buur na, ki bonboorii na yenninba yat ki baa sanjouk paak, ki nɔɔnii wei ki tɔkir.
translated: They went to the farm, and the farmer gave them a small basket of corn, and they ate and were satisfied.
